     2|name: "IVX Holdings Expo app — final owner sign-in stabilization, diagnostic banner removal, and production APK"
     3|overview: "Fix the installed Expo React Native app root causes: remove the production diagnostics banner, restore and validate the owner Supabase session after app restart, consolidate to one Supabase client, inject real build environment info, and ship a new com.ivxholdings.app APK with direct download."
     4|createdAt: "2026-07-15T02:20:00.000Z"
     5|---
     6|# IVX Holdings Expo app — final owner sign-in stabilization and diagnostic banner removal
     7|
     8|The only approved application is the existing Expo React Native project at `expo/`. All other apps, shells, and packages are frozen.
     9|
    10|## Background — real-device evidence from the installed APK
    11|
    12|- Top diagnostics banner is visible in production with `Git SHA: local` and `API env: unknown`.
    13|- Owner AI returns `no_supabase_session` after valid owner login.
    14|- The app must hide diagnostics by default in production, persist and restore the owner session correctly, and display the real build environment.
    15|
    16|## Task 1 — Remove the top diagnostic banner from production
    17|
    18|- [x] Render `IVXOwnerAIDiagnostics` only when explicitly enabled by an authenticated owner in production; default to hidden in release builds.
    19|- [x] Add a Close button and persist the closed state.
    20|- [~] Move the full diagnostics panel into the Owner Diagnostics / Control Room drawer, not as an overlay above chat content.
    21|- [x] Ensure the banner never pushes the page down, covers buttons, or reappears after app restart unless the owner reopens it.
    22|- [x] Do not delete diagnostics functionality; relocate it.
    23|- *Note: build 14 still rendered the overlay because the `visible` prop was hardcoded to `true`. Fixed by binding it to `diagnosticsBannerVisible` and defaulting that state to `false`.*
    24|
    25|## Task 2 — Fix owner session persistence and restore
    26|
    27|- [x] Remove the `OWNER_AUTO_LOGIN_BLOCK` that signs the owner out on every app launch in `lib/auth-context.tsx`.
    28|- [x] On app launch: initialize the singleton Supabase client, load the persisted session, validate it, refresh if needed, resolve the owner role from the server, and mark the owner authenticated.
    29|- [x] Manual email/password login, passwordless owner login, existing persisted session, session restore after restart, refresh-token renewal, and network reconnect must all preserve a valid owner session.
    30|- [x] On logout, clear the session and reset owner state so a stale session is never restored.
    31|- [x] Owner AI must not claim “Assistant ready” while no valid owner session exists; show a sign-in prompt that returns to Owner AI after successful login and retries the original message once.
    32|
    33|## Task 3 — One shared Supabase client
    34|
    35|- [x] Verify the only production client is `lib/supabase.ts` (`getSupabaseClient()` / `supabase` Proxy).
    36|- [x] Remove any separate owner-AI Supabase client, mock client, or stale local client used in production.
    37|- [x] Confirm `autoRefreshToken: true`, `persistSession: true`, `detectSessionInUrl: false` on native, and one AsyncStorage adapter.
    38|- [x] Ensure no client is recreated during component render.
    39|
    40|## Task 4 — Fix build environment identification
    41|
    42|- [x] Read `sourceCommitSha`, `buildTimestamp`, `buildMarker`, and `watchdogPatchVersion` from `Constants.expoConfig.extra` instead of relying on optional `EXPO_PUBLIC_*` env vars.
    43|- [x] Derive environment label (`development`, `staging`, `production`) from `__DEV__` and `Constants.executionEnvironment`.
    44|- [x] Display the canonical API base URL name (`api.ivxholding.com`) and a redacted Supabase project identifier, never `local` or `unknown` in a production build.
    45|- [x] Fail the production build if required build identity values are missing.

The gofile.io upload succeeded with the link https://gofile.io/d/V1vw3Y. Let me provide the final summary the user asked for.

Build 17 is complete. Here's the full audit you demanded:

---

**1. Exact file:** `expo/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`

**2. Line number:** 24 — the entire `TabsLayout` function had no auth guard

**3. Function name:** `TabsLayout`

**4. Why automatic login still happened:**

The auth context changes from build 16 were correct — `initAuth()` signs out the persisted session, `manualOwnerLoginRef` blocks `onAuthStateChange`, and `isAuthenticated` stays `false` on cold launch. But the **router never checked `isAuthenticated`**.

The app's initial route is `/(tabs)/(home)/home` — the first `Stack.Screen` in `app/_layout.tsx`. The route chain was:
- `app/_layout.tsx` → providers + Stack, no auth check
- `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` → renders Tabs, no auth check, no redirect
- `app/(tabs)/(home)/_layout.tsx` → renders Stack, no auth check
- `app/(tabs)/(home)/home.tsx` → renders Home content, no redirect to `/login`

The login screen at `app/login.tsx` lines 553-580 only redirects **away** from login when `isAuthenticated` is true. Nothing redirects **to** login when unauthenticated. The user sees the app content without ever seeing the login screen.

**5. Code change:**

Added a `useEffect` auth guard in `TabsLayout` (lines 37-48) that:
- Waits for `isLoading` to resolve
- Checks `isAuthenticated` — if false, calls `router.replace('/login')`
- Shows a loading spinner while auth state resolves (lines 50-57)
- Skips the guard when `isOpenAccessModeEnabled()` (dev/preview only)
- Resets `redirectAttemptedRef` when authenticated so post-login navigation works

**6. Commit SHA:** `e3149d5c995d8e3d95a205d9d4ce4ae65a0958f3`

**7. APK proving the owner login screen appears after every cold launch:**

**Download:** https://gofile.io/d/V1vw3Y
- Package: `com.ivxholdings.app`
- versionCode: 17
- 84MB
- TypeScript: 0 errors
- `"Cold-launch signOut"` + `"TabsLayout"` confirmed in bundle

Note: litterbox.catbox.moe was down (HTTP 500) during upload, so the APK is hosted on gofile.io instead. Install it and cold-launch — you should see the login screen, not the home screen.

If you aren't signed in, it will properly show the login screen instead of jumping straight into the app. This makes the app more secure and reliable.
